text.title: Agent Customer-Zero Pilot Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-customer-zero-pilot-protocol enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Pilot scope is bounded: ≤3 use cases, single tenant, time-boxed to 12 weeks.
rule.rationale: Unbounded scope expands to GA without the gate; 12-week box forces a real decision.

rule.statement: Success criteria have numeric thresholds defined before pilot starts (e.g., ≥85% eval score, ≤$X/call, <5% intervention rate).
rule.rationale: Pilots without numeric gates always 'succeed' qualitatively; numeric gates make 'sunset' a real option.

rule.statement: Kill switch is wired and tested on day 0; not as a week-12 deliverable.
rule.rationale: Late kill switches are theatre; day-0 test catches the wiring bugs before the agent has user impact.

rule.statement: Billing model decided up-front: free pilot OR paid pilot with credit OR full-rate; never bill retroactively.
rule.rationale: Retroactive billing destroys customer trust faster than any product failure.

rule.statement: Mid-pilot review at week 4: green-light to continue, amber to course-correct, red to sunset. No silent extensions.
rule.rationale: Without a check-in, failing pilots drift to the wall; an explicit check-in lets the team sunset gracefully.
